title: How to Reset Your Active Directory Password
description: Step-by-step guide for resetting your AD password without IT assistance.
content: To reset your Active Directory password: 1) Press Ctrl+Alt+Delete and select Change a Password. 2) Enter your current password. 3) Enter your new password twice. Passwords must be at least 12 characters and include uppercase letters numbers and symbols. If you are locked out contact the IT helpdesk at ext 4357.

title: VPN Setup Guide for Remote Workers
description: Complete instructions for configuring the corporate VPN on Windows and macOS.
content: Install the GlobalProtect VPN client from the IT portal. On Windows run the installer and follow the prompts. On macOS drag the app to Applications. Launch GlobalProtect and enter portal address vpn.company.com. Use your corporate email and password. For MFA approve the push notification on your mobile device. Contact IT if you experience connection timeouts.

title: Company Expense Reimbursement Policy
description: Official policy for submitting and approving business expense claims.
content: All business expenses must be submitted within 30 days of incurrence via the Concur expense system. Receipts are required for amounts over $25. Meal expenses are capped at $75 per person per day. Travel requires pre-approval for trips over $500. Submit your report to your direct manager for approval. Finance processes reimbursements within 10 business days of approval.

title: Annual Performance Review Process
description: Guide to preparing for and completing the annual performance review cycle.
content: Performance reviews occur in Q4 each year. Employees complete a self-assessment in Workday by November 1st. Managers conduct 1:1 meetings to discuss goals and achievements. Ratings range from 1-5 where 3 is meets expectations. Reviews are finalized by December 15th. Compensation adjustments take effect January 1st. HR provides training workshops each October to support the process.

title: Onboarding Checklist for New Hires
description: Everything a new employee needs to complete in their first 30 days.
content: Week 1: Complete I-9 and tax forms in Workday. Attend new hire orientation on Day 1. Set up your laptop using the IT setup guide. Get your access badge from reception. Week 2: Complete mandatory compliance training in LMS. Meet with your manager to align on 30-60-90 day goals. Week 3-4: Shadow team members. Complete role-specific training modules. Schedule intro meetings with key stakeholders.

title: Information Security Awareness Training
description: Required annual training covering phishing social engineering and data protection.
content: This training must be completed annually by all employees. Topics covered include phishing email identification social engineering tactics password hygiene data classification and incident reporting. Training takes approximately 2 hours and is available in the LMS. Employees who do not complete training by the deadline will have system access suspended. Contact security@company.com for questions.

title: How to Submit a Helpdesk Ticket
description: Instructions for logging IT support requests through the ServiceNow portal.
content: Navigate to helpdesk.company.com and click New Ticket. Select the appropriate category such as Hardware Software or Access. Provide a clear description of your issue including error messages and steps to reproduce. Set priority to Critical only for production outages. You will receive an email confirmation with a ticket number. Average response time is 4 hours for standard requests and 30 minutes for critical issues.

title: Remote Work Policy and Best Practices
description: Company guidelines for employees working from home or remote locations.
content: Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week with manager approval. Remote workers must maintain core hours of 10am-3pm in their local timezone. Use the corporate VPN when accessing company resources. Ensure your home workspace meets ergonomic standards. Attend all video calls with camera on. Notify your manager of any schedule changes. Fully remote roles require VP approval and HR documentation.

title: Product Roadmap Q1 2025
description: Overview of planned product features and releases for Q1 2025.
content: Q1 2025 roadmap includes three major initiatives. Feature 1: Advanced search with AI-powered recommendations targeting February release. Feature 2: Mobile app redesign for iOS and Android targeting March release. Feature 3: API rate limiting enhancements for enterprise customers targeting January release. All features are subject to change. Product reviews occur bi-weekly on Tuesdays. Reach out to product@company.com for input.

title: Git Branching Strategy and Workflow
description: Engineering team guidelines for managing code branches and pull requests.
content: We use trunk-based development with short-lived feature branches. Branch names follow the pattern feature/TICKET-description or bugfix/TICKET-description. All changes require a pull request with at least one reviewer approval. PRs must pass CI checks before merging. Main branch is always deployable. Releases are tagged with semantic versioning. Hotfixes branch from main and are merged back to both main and the current release branch.

title: Leave and Absence Policy
description: Comprehensive guide to vacation sick leave and other time-off policies.
content: Employees accrue 1.25 vacation days per month up to a maximum of 20 days. Sick leave is granted at 10 days per year and does not carry over. Parental leave provides 12 weeks fully paid for primary caregivers and 4 weeks for secondary caregivers. Personal days: 3 per year non-accruing. Submit leave requests in Workday at least 2 weeks in advance for vacation. Sick leave can be reported same-day by notifying your manager.

title: Data Classification and Handling Policy
description: How to classify and protect company and customer data assets.
content: Data is classified into four levels. Public: no restrictions. Internal: for employees only. Confidential: business sensitive data requiring encryption. Restricted: PII and financial data requiring strict access controls. All employee laptops must use full-disk encryption. Customer data must never be stored on personal devices. Sharing confidential data externally requires DLP approval. Violations must be reported to the security team immediately.

title: API Documentation Standards
description: Engineering standards for writing and maintaining API documentation.
content: All public APIs must have OpenAPI 3.0 compliant documentation. Document every endpoint with description parameters request body and response schemas. Include example requests and responses. Mark deprecated endpoints with a deprecation date. Version your APIs using URL path versioning such as /v1/. Update documentation as part of every PR that changes API behaviour. Use the internal API portal to publish docs.

title: Benefits Enrollment Guide
description: How to enroll in or change health dental and vision benefits.
content: Open enrollment occurs each November for the following calendar year. Log in to BenefitsFocus at benefits.company.com. Review your current coverage and make changes during the enrollment window. Qualifying life events such as marriage or new dependents allow changes outside open enrollment. Submit documentation within 30 days of the qualifying event. Contact HR at hr@company.com for assistance. Benefit changes take effect January 1st or the first of the month after a qualifying event.

title: Incident Response Playbook
description: Steps to follow when a security incident is detected or suspected.
content: Step 1 Identify: Document what you observed including timestamps and affected systems. Step 2 Contain: Isolate affected systems from the network if possible. Step 3 Report: Immediately notify security@company.com and your manager. Step 4 Assess: Work with the security team to determine scope and impact. Step 5 Eradicate: Remove the threat and patch vulnerabilities. Step 6 Recover: Restore systems from clean backups. Step 7 Post-Mortem: Document lessons learned within 5 business days.

title: Code Review Guidelines
description: Best practices for reviewing and providing feedback on pull requests.
content: Code reviews improve quality and share knowledge. Reviewers should focus on logic correctness performance and security not style (use linters for that). Provide specific actionable feedback. Avoid personal criticism. Approve if the code is good enough even if not perfect. Authors should respond to all comments before merging. Reviews should be completed within 1 business day. Use the Conventional Comments format for structured feedback.

title: Travel Booking and Policy
description: How to book business travel and understand reimbursable expenses.
content: Book all business travel through Concur Travel. Flights should be economy class for domestic and premium economy for international over 6 hours. Book hotels through preferred vendors for negotiated rates. Car rentals require collision damage waiver coverage. Per diem rates are based on the GSA schedule for domestic travel. Submit all travel expenses within 30 days of return. Pre-approval is required for international travel and any trip over $1000.

title: Docker and Container Best Practices
description: Guidelines for building and running containerized applications.
content: Use official base images and pin to specific versions. Keep images small using multi-stage builds. Never run containers as root. Store secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager not in images. Health checks should be defined for all production containers. Use .dockerignore to exclude unnecessary files. Tag images with the git commit SHA. Scan images for vulnerabilities using Trivy as part of CI.

title: New Employee Benefits Overview
description: Summary of all benefits available to employees from day one.
content: From Day 1 you receive health dental and vision insurance. Health plan options include PPO HMO and HDHP. The company contributes 80% of premiums. 401k with 4% company match vests immediately. Life insurance at 2x annual salary. EAP counseling services 8 free sessions per year. Gym reimbursement up to $50 per month. Learning budget of $2000 per year. Commuter benefits up to $315 per month pre-tax.

title: Phishing Email Recognition Guide
description: How to identify and report phishing and social engineering attempts.
content: Phishing emails often display urgency and threaten account suspension. Check the sender email domain carefully for spoofing. Hover over links to preview the URL before clicking. Legitimate companies never ask for passwords via email. Be wary of unexpected attachments especially ZIP and Office files with macros. If in doubt forward the email to phishing@company.com. Do not click any links or download attachments from suspicious messages.

title: Database Backup and Recovery Procedures
description: Standard operating procedures for database backups and disaster recovery.
content: Production databases are backed up daily with full backups on Sundays and incrementals Monday through Saturday. Backups are stored in S3 with cross-region replication. Retention is 30 days for daily backups and 1 year for monthly snapshots. Recovery time objective is 4 hours. Test restores are performed quarterly. For a recovery request open a Sev-1 helpdesk ticket. DBA on-call handles all production recovery events.

title: Workplace Harassment Prevention Policy
description: Company policy on preventing and reporting workplace harassment.
content: The company maintains a zero-tolerance policy for harassment of any kind. Harassment includes verbal physical and visual conduct that creates a hostile work environment. Report incidents to HR directly or via the anonymous hotline at 1-800-555-0199. All reports are investigated promptly and confidentially. Retaliation against anyone who reports harassment is prohibited. Managers who witness harassment must report it. Annual training is mandatory for all employees.

title: Kubernetes Cluster Management Guide
description: Operational guide for managing production Kubernetes clusters.
content: Use kubectl with the approved kubeconfig context for your environment. Never run kubectl delete on production without a change request. Monitor cluster health via the Grafana dashboard at grafana.internal. Node autoscaling is enabled with min 3 max 20 nodes per availability zone. Deploy using Helm charts versioned in the platform repository. Log aggregation uses the ELK stack. Runbooks for common incidents are in the Engineering Wiki.

title: Product Launch Checklist
description: Steps required before launching a new product or major feature.
content: Two weeks before launch: complete user acceptance testing. finalize documentation and help articles. One week before: send launch announcement to internal stakeholders. prepare customer communication. Confirm monitoring alerts are configured. Day of launch: deploy during off-peak hours. monitor error rates for 2 hours post-deploy. Confirm feature flags are enabled for target segments. Post-launch: review metrics at 24h and 7d. Conduct retrospective within 2 weeks.

title: Finance Month-End Close Process
description: Steps the finance team follows to close the books each month.
content: The month-end close follows a 5-day schedule. Day 1: Record all journal entries and accruals. Day 2: Reconcile all balance sheet accounts. Day 3: Review P&L for anomalies and obtain department sign-offs. Day 4: Consolidate subsidiaries and intercompany eliminations. Day 5: Finalize trial balance and submit management reports. All non-standard journal entries require controller approval. Close calendar is distributed by the CFO office on the first of each month.

title: SSL Certificate Management
description: How to manage renew and monitor SSL/TLS certificates.
content: All SSL certificates are managed in CertManager. Certificates expire alerts fire at 30 days and 7 days. Renew certificates using the automated script in the devops repository. For certificates requiring manual renewal open a change request ticket 60 days before expiry. Wildcard certificates require CISO approval. Store private keys in HashiCorp Vault never in code repositories. Run the certificate inventory script quarterly to ensure no expirations are missed.

title: Agile Sprint Planning Guide
description: How to run effective sprint planning meetings and estimate work.
content: Sprint planning occurs every two weeks on Monday morning. The team reviews the product backlog and selects items for the sprint. Stories are estimated using story points with the Fibonacci scale. Use planning poker to achieve consensus. Velocity is the average of the last 3 sprints. Do not over-commit: use 80% of velocity as the sprint target. Define the sprint goal before ending the meeting. Planning should not exceed 2 hours for a 2-week sprint.

title: Company Code of Conduct
description: Standards of professional behaviour expected of all employees.
content: Employees are expected to act with integrity honesty and respect. Conflicts of interest must be disclosed to HR immediately. Do not accept gifts valued over $50 from vendors or clients. Company assets including equipment and time must not be used for personal gain. Confidential information must be protected. Violations of the code of conduct may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. Report concerns to your manager HR or the ethics hotline.

title: Monitoring and Alerting Setup
description: Guide to configuring monitoring and alerts for production services.
content: All production services must have health check endpoints. Configure Prometheus scraping in the service manifest. Alerts are managed in PagerDuty. On-call rotation is managed via the PagerDuty schedule. Critical alerts page immediately. Warning alerts create a ticket in Jira. Set up dashboards in Grafana for each service. Alert thresholds: error rate above 1% CPU above 80% memory above 90% latency p99 above 2 seconds.

title: Employee Stock Options (ESOP) Guide
description: How the employee stock option plan works including vesting and exercise.
content: All full-time employees receive stock options at hire. The standard grant vests over 4 years with a 1-year cliff. Your option grant and strike price are in your offer letter. Options can be exercised within 90 days of leaving the company. Log in to Carta to view your equity dashboard. Consult a tax advisor before exercising options as tax implications vary. Annual grants are made at the Q1 board meeting based on performance.

title: Disaster Recovery Plan
description: Business continuity and disaster recovery procedures for critical systems.
content: RPO and RTO targets are defined per service tier. Tier 1 critical systems have 1-hour RTO and 15-minute RPO. Tier 2 important systems have 4-hour RTO and 1-hour RPO. Tier 3 non-critical have 24-hour RTO. DR drills are conducted quarterly. Failover to the secondary region is automated for Tier 1 services. DR runbooks are maintained in Confluence. The DR coordinator role rotates monthly among senior engineers.

title: Hiring Manager Interview Guide
description: How to conduct structured interviews and evaluate candidates fairly.
content: All interviews must use structured behavioural questions from the approved question bank. Avoid questions about age family status religion or other protected characteristics. Score candidates on a 1-4 scale for each competency. Debrief within 24 hours of interviews while feedback is fresh. Use the Greenhouse platform to record all feedback. Hiring decisions must be unanimous among core interviewers. Extend offers through the recruiter never directly to the candidate.

title: Feature Flag Management
description: How to use and manage feature flags in production applications.
content: Feature flags are managed via LaunchDarkly. Create flags using the naming convention project_feature_description. Always clean up flags within 30 days of full rollout. Never hard-code flag keys in client code use the SDK constants. Test flag variations in staging before production. Use percentage rollouts for gradual releases. Monitor flag usage with the LaunchDarkly dashboard. Document the purpose and owner of every flag.

title: Payroll Processing Schedule
description: Dates and procedures for the bi-weekly payroll cycle.
content: Payroll is processed bi-weekly on Fridays. The pay period ends on Sunday and is processed Wednesday for Friday deposit. Submit timesheets by Monday 5pm for the previous pay period. Corrections to missed time must be submitted as a Payroll Adjustment form to HR. Direct deposit takes effect within 2 banking days. Off-cycle payrolls for terminations are processed within 3 business days. Contact payroll@company.com for any discrepancies.

title: Zero Trust Network Architecture
description: Overview of the company zero trust security model and implementation.
content: Zero Trust assumes no implicit trust for any user or device on any network. All access requires authentication even on the corporate network. We implement Zero Trust via BeyondCorp principles. Devices must be enrolled in MDM to access corporate resources. Application access is granted by role using our IAM platform. Network segmentation prevents lateral movement. All traffic is encrypted in transit. Anomalous access patterns trigger automated alerts.

title: Onboarding Week 1 IT Setup
description: Detailed IT setup steps for your first week at the company.
content: Day 1 Morning: Collect your laptop from IT on floor 3. Sign the equipment form. Day 1 Afternoon: Run the company setup script from the IT portal. This installs required software and configures your machine policies. Day 2: Set up your corporate email in Outlook and configure MFA. Day 3: Connect to the corporate VPN. Install Slack and join your team channels. Day 4: Set up access to GitHub Jira and Confluence using SSO. Day 5: Complete the IT security training module in the LMS.

title: Microservices Design Principles
description: Guidelines for designing and building microservices at the company.
content: Each service owns its data and has a single responsibility. Services communicate via REST or gRPC. Use async messaging via Kafka for event-driven patterns. Services must expose health readiness and liveness endpoints. Design for failure using circuit breakers and retries with exponential backoff. Version APIs and maintain backward compatibility. Each service has its own deployment pipeline. Service mesh (Istio) handles mTLS between services.

title: Acceptable Use Policy for Company Equipment
description: Rules governing the use of company-issued devices and systems.
content: Company equipment is provided for business use. Limited personal use is permitted. Do not install unauthorized software. Do not disable security tools including antivirus and MDM. Equipment must not be lent to third parties. Report lost or stolen devices to IT immediately via the security hotline. All company devices are subject to monitoring. Data on company devices is company property. Remote wipe may be performed on lost devices without notice.

title: Product Feedback and Feature Requests
description: How customers and employees can submit product feedback and feature requests.
content: Submit feature requests via the product feedback portal at feedback.company.com. Internal employees use the #product-feedback Slack channel. Requests are reviewed weekly by the product team. Upvote existing requests rather than creating duplicates. High-vote requests are added to the product backlog for consideration. You will be notified when your request changes status. Customer-facing requests from sales are escalated via the Customer Success team.

title: Secure Coding Practices
description: Developer guidelines for writing secure code and avoiding common vulnerabilities.
content: Validate all input on the server side never trust client data. Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Encode output to prevent XSS. Store passwords using bcrypt with cost factor 12 or higher. Implement CSRF protection on all state-changing endpoints. Use HTTPS everywhere. Apply principle of least privilege for service accounts. Conduct security code reviews for authentication authorization and data handling code. Run OWASP dependency checks in CI.

title: Accounts Payable Process
description: How to submit and approve vendor invoices for payment.
content: Submit vendor invoices to ap@company.com with the PO number in the subject line. Invoices without a valid PO will be returned. Three-way matching: invoice must match PO and goods receipt. Approval thresholds: under $5000 manager approval. $5000-$50000 director approval. Over $50000 VP and Finance approval. Payment terms are Net 30 by default. Preferred vendors receive Net 45 with 2% early payment discount. Run the AP aging report weekly to identify overdue items.

title: Cloud Cost Optimization Guide
description: Strategies for managing and reducing cloud infrastructure costs.
content: Review the monthly AWS Cost Explorer report shared by FinOps. Rightsize EC2 instances using the Compute Optimizer recommendations. Delete unattached EBS volumes and unused Elastic IPs monthly. Use Reserved Instances for stable workloads to save up to 40%. Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering for infrequently accessed data. Set budget alerts in AWS Budgets. All new cloud resources require tagging with project environment and owner. FinOps reviews are held quarterly.

title: Diversity Equity and Inclusion Policy
description: Company commitment to building a diverse and inclusive workplace.
content: The company is committed to equal opportunity employment. We do not discriminate based on race gender age sexual orientation disability or any other protected characteristic. ERGs (Employee Resource Groups) are supported by executive sponsors. Unconscious bias training is required annually. Compensation equity audits are conducted annually. Report DEI concerns to HR or the anonymous hotline. The DEI dashboard is published to all employees quarterly.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Overview
description: How the continuous integration and deployment pipeline works.
content: Code pushed to a feature branch triggers unit tests and linting. Merging to main triggers the full pipeline including integration tests security scans and build. Deployment to staging is automatic on main merge. Production deployments require a manual approval gate in GitHub Actions. Deployments use blue-green strategy for zero downtime. Rollback is triggered automatically if error rate exceeds 2% post-deploy. Deployment logs are retained for 90 days.

title: Customer Data Protection Standards
description: How to handle and protect customer personal and financial data.
content: Customer PII must be encrypted at rest and in transit. Access to customer data requires explicit business justification and manager approval. Customer data must not be copied to personal devices or unauthorized systems. Data retention periods: active customers indefinite. Churned customers 7 years. Anonymize or delete data per the customer data deletion request process within 30 days. Conduct a DPIA for any new processing of sensitive customer data.

title: How to Use the Internal LMS
description: Guide for accessing and completing training courses in the learning management system.
content: Log in to the LMS at learn.company.com using your SSO credentials. Browse the course catalog or search by topic. Required courses appear in your My Learning dashboard with due dates. Click Enroll to add optional courses. Courses auto-save your progress. Download certificates from the Completed tab. Managers can view team completion rates in the Manager Dashboard. Contact training@company.com to request new courses or report technical issues.

title: Infrastructure as Code with Terraform
description: How to manage cloud infrastructure using Terraform.
content: All cloud infrastructure is managed as code in the infra repository. Use modules from the internal Terraform registry. Run terraform plan before apply and review all changes. Changes to production require peer review and change management approval. State is stored in S3 with DynamoDB locking. Never run terraform destroy on production. Tag all resources using the tagging module. Modules are versioned use pinned version constraints.

title: Internal Transfer Policy
description: Process for requesting and approving internal role transfers.
content: Employees may apply for internal transfers after 12 months in their current role. Discuss your interest with your current manager before applying. Apply through the internal job board in Workday. The hiring manager conducts interviews. If selected an agreed transition period of 4-8 weeks is standard. Compensation changes follow the standard promotion or lateral transfer guidelines. HR facilitates the process and ensures all parties agree on the timeline.

title: API Rate Limiting and Throttling
description: How rate limits are applied to the public API and what to do when limits are hit.
content: API rate limits apply per API key. Free tier: 100 requests per minute. Standard: 1000 per minute. Enterprise: 10000 per minute. Rate limit headers X-RateLimit-Limit X-RateLimit-Remaining and X-RateLimit-Reset are returned on every response. When the limit is exceeded a 429 Too Many Requests response is returned. Implement exponential backoff with jitter in your client. Contact sales@company.com to discuss higher limits.

title: Annual Compliance Training Requirements
description: Mandatory training all employees must complete each year.
content: The following training modules are required annually: Information Security Awareness (2h). Code of Conduct (1h). Anti-Bribery and Corruption (1h). Data Privacy and GDPR (1h). Workplace Safety (30 min). Due dates are set to December 31st each year. Non-completion results in system access restrictions. Completion status is tracked in the LMS and reported to department heads monthly. New hires must complete all modules within 30 days of start date.

title: Vendor Management Policy
description: How to onboard evaluate and manage third-party vendors.
content: New vendors must complete the vendor onboarding form and provide business insurance and SOC 2 report. Vendors handling company data must sign a DPA. Annual vendor reviews evaluate performance SLA compliance and financial stability. Preferred vendor status requires executive sponsorship. Contracts are managed in the CLM system. Changes to vendor scope require a contract amendment. Off-board vendors by revoking all system access within 24 hours of contract end.

title: Terraform Module Development Guide
description: How to write and publish reusable Terraform modules for the internal registry.
content: Modules must have a README inputs outputs and examples directory. Use semantic versioning for module releases. Inputs must have type constraints and descriptions. Sensitive outputs must be marked sensitive. Include validation blocks for complex input constraints. Test modules using Terratest. Submit modules via pull request to the infra-modules repository. The platform team reviews and publishes approved modules to the internal registry.

title: Mental Health and Wellbeing Resources
description: Company-provided mental health resources and support programmes.
content: The company offers 8 free EAP counselling sessions per year through LifeWorks. Access via the EAP portal at eap.company.com or call 1-800-555-0100 available 24/7. Counsellors are available for personal work and family issues. Manager support training is offered quarterly. The Wellbeing Committee hosts monthly events including meditation sessions and lunch-and-learns. If you are in crisis call 988 the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline. Your use of EAP services is confidential.

title: Payment Processing and PCI Compliance
description: Requirements for systems that handle payment card data.
content: All payment card data must be processed through our PCI-compliant payment gateway. Never store full card numbers CVV codes or PIN data. Tokenization is used for recurring payments. Systems in scope for PCI must be in the CDE network segment. Quarterly vulnerability scans are required for all in-scope systems. Annual PCI DSS self-assessment questionnaire is completed by IT Security. Report any suspected card data breach to security@company.com immediately.

title: Product Pricing and Packaging
description: Current product pricing tiers and what is included in each plan.
content: Starter plan: $49/month for up to 5 users. Basic features included. Growth plan: $199/month for up to 25 users. Advanced analytics and integrations. Enterprise plan: custom pricing for unlimited users with SLA SSO and dedicated support. All plans include unlimited storage and 99.9% uptime SLA. Annual billing provides a 20% discount. Non-profit and educational discounts of 50% are available. Contact sales@company.com for volume pricing.

title: SQL Query Optimization Tips
description: Best practices for writing high-performance SQL queries.
content: Always use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to profile queries before optimizing. Avoid SELECT * specify only needed columns. Use indexes for columns in WHERE JOIN and ORDER BY clauses. Avoid functions on indexed columns in WHERE conditions. Use EXISTS instead of IN for subqueries with large datasets. Paginate large result sets with LIMIT and OFFSET or keyset pagination. Avoid N+1 queries by using JOIN or eager loading. Review slow query logs weekly in the DBA dashboard.

title: Quarterly Business Review Process
description: How QBRs are structured and what each team should prepare.
content: QBRs are held in the last week of each quarter. Each department presents: KPIs vs targets. Key achievements and misses. Risks and blockers for the next quarter. Resource requests. Presentations are 20 minutes with 10 minutes of Q&A. Slides must be submitted to the EA office 5 business days before the review. Action items from QBRs are tracked in the company OKR system.

title: Logging Standards for Applications
description: How application logs should be structured and stored.
content: Use structured JSON logging for all services. Required fields: timestamp (ISO 8601) level service_name message request_id. Log levels: ERROR for failures WARN for degraded behaviour INFO for normal operations DEBUG for development only. Do not log passwords tokens or PII. Logs are shipped to Elasticsearch via Fluentd. Retention is 30 days for INFO and 90 days for ERROR and WARN. Query logs in Kibana at logs.internal.

title: Employee Referral Program
description: How to refer candidates and earn referral bonuses.
content: Refer a candidate by submitting their resume to referrals@company.com with the job title. Use your employee ID in the subject line. Referrals are tracked in Greenhouse. Referral bonus of $2000 is paid after the referred employee completes 90 days. Bonus is paid in the next payroll cycle. Employees may not refer candidates for roles they directly manage. Refer as many candidates as you like. There is no cap on referral bonuses.

title: Cloud Security Posture Management
description: How we monitor and enforce security standards across cloud accounts.
content: We use AWS Security Hub and Prisma Cloud for CSPM. All findings above medium severity must be remediated within 30 days. Critical findings must be remediated within 72 hours. Findings are triaged weekly by the cloud security team. New AWS accounts must pass the security baseline check before production use. IaC scanning is integrated into CI to catch misconfigurations before deployment. Monthly CSPM reports are shared with the CISO.

title: Account Receivable Collections Process
description: How the AR team manages overdue invoices and collections.
content: Invoices become overdue at Net 30. Day 31: Automated reminder email sent. Day 45: Account manager notified for customer outreach. Day 60: Formal collections letter sent. Day 90: Account placed on credit hold. New orders blocked until balance paid. Day 120: Referred to third-party collections agency. Write-off requires VP Finance approval. All collection communications are logged in Salesforce. Payment plans may be offered with CFO approval for balances over $10000.

title: Onboarding Buddy Programme
description: How the buddy system works to support new hire integration.
content: Each new hire is paired with an onboarding buddy for their first 90 days. Buddies are volunteers from the same or similar team. Buddies check in weekly to answer questions provide context and make introductions. Buddy responsibilities include lunch on Day 1. Tour of the office. Introductions to key colleagues. Sharing team norms and culture. Nominate yourself as a buddy via the HR portal. Buddies receive recognition in the company newsletter.

title: Penetration Testing Policy
description: How and when penetration tests are conducted on company systems.
content: Annual penetration tests are conducted by an approved third-party firm. Scope covers external perimeter internal network web applications and APIs. Pre-engagement: define scope rules of engagement and emergency contacts. During testing: the security team monitors for anomalies. Post-engagement: findings are reported in a CVSS-scored report. Critical and high findings must be remediated within 30 and 90 days respectively. Retests confirm remediation. Results are reviewed by the CISO and CTO.

title: Engineering On-Call Runbook
description: How to handle the on-call rotation and respond to incidents.
content: On-call rotation is weekly starting Monday at 9am. Review the PagerDuty schedule at the start of your rotation. For each alert assess severity using the SEV 1-4 scale. SEV-1 page the escalation list and start a war room in Slack. SEV-2 investigate and resolve within 2 hours. SEV-3 create a Jira ticket and resolve within 1 business day. SEV-4 create a ticket for backlog triage. After an incident file a post-mortem within 5 days.

title: Contract Review and Approval Process
description: Legal procedure for reviewing and signing contracts.
content: All contracts must be reviewed by Legal before signing. Submit contracts to contracts@company.com with the business context. Turnaround is 5 business days for standard contracts and 10 days for complex agreements. Signing authority: under $50000 VP or above. Over $50000 C-suite only. Use the approved contract templates for NDAs MSAs and SOWs available in the Legal SharePoint. Never use a vendor's paper without Legal review. Executed contracts are stored in the CLM system.

title: Accessibility Standards for Digital Products
description: How to build accessible digital products compliant with WCAG 2.1.
content: All customer-facing products must meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA. Use semantic HTML elements. Provide alt text for all images. Ensure keyboard navigability for all interactive elements. Colour contrast ratio must be at least 4.5:1 for normal text. Support screen readers by testing with NVDA and VoiceOver. Include captions for all video content. Conduct accessibility audits using axe DevTools before each major release.

title: Workplace Health and Safety Policy
description: Company commitment to maintaining a safe workplace for all employees.
content: Report hazards and near-misses using the safety reporting form in the employee portal. First aid kits are located at each floor reception. Fire wardens are assigned per floor (see posted list). Ergonomic assessments are available by request through HR. Personal protective equipment is provided for roles requiring it. Alcohol and substance use at work are prohibited. Safety drills are conducted twice yearly. Violations of safety standards must be reported to the Facilities team.

title: Kubernetes Deployment Best Practices
description: How to configure and deploy applications to the Kubernetes cluster.
content: Define resource requests and limits for every container. Use liveness and readiness probes. Set replicas to at least 2 for production workloads. Use PodDisruptionBudgets to ensure availability during node maintenance. Store secrets in Kubernetes Secrets or Vault never in ConfigMaps. Use namespaces to separate environments. Apply NetworkPolicies to restrict pod communication. Use HorizontalPodAutoscaler for workloads with variable load. Review deployment manifests in the platform repository.

title: Anti-Bribery and Corruption Policy
description: Company policy prohibiting bribery facilitation payments and improper gifts.
content: The company prohibits all forms of bribery and corruption. Never offer or accept anything of value to influence a business decision. Gifts over $50 must be reported to your manager. Facilitation payments are prohibited even where locally customary. Due diligence on third parties must be completed before engagement. Training is required annually. Violations must be reported to Legal or the ethics hotline immediately. Violations will result in termination and may result in criminal prosecution.

title: Customer Success Escalation Process
description: How customer issues are escalated from support to engineering.
content: Level 1 support handles common issues using the knowledge base. If unresolved after 2 hours or by Level 1 escalate to Level 2. Level 2 investigates with system access. If a bug is confirmed create a Jira bug ticket with SEV rating. SEV-1 customer-blocking bugs page the on-call engineer. SEV-2 bugs are prioritised in the next sprint. P1 customers get a status update every 2 hours. RCA is shared with the customer within 5 business days of resolution.

title: How to Write a Post-Mortem
description: Template and guidance for documenting incidents and preventing recurrence.
content: Post-mortems are blameless. Focus on systems and processes not individuals. Required sections: Summary. Timeline of events. Root cause analysis (use the 5 Whys). Impact assessment. Remediation actions with owners and due dates. Action items are tracked in Jira. Post-mortems must be completed within 5 business days. They are reviewed in the weekly engineering meeting. High-severity post-mortems are shared with leadership.

title: Stock Compensation and Tax Implications
description: Understanding the tax treatment of RSUs options and ESPP.
content: RSUs are taxed as ordinary income when they vest. The vested value is included in your W-2. Consult a tax advisor about estimated quarterly tax payments if your RSU income is significant. ISOs have more favourable tax treatment but require careful timing of exercise and sale. NSOs are taxed on exercise as ordinary income. ESPP shares sold within 2 years of the offering date are a disqualifying disposition. Carta provides year-end tax documents by January 31st.

title: Network Access Control Policy
description: Who can access the corporate network and under what conditions.
content: Corporate network access requires an enrolled corporate device or approved BYOD device. Guest network is available for visitors and personal devices. Network zones: Corporate (employees) DMZ (internet-facing services) Production (restricted to ops team) Management (infrastructure team only). VPN access is required for remote work. Network access logs are retained for 12 months. Unauthorised access attempts trigger an automatic security alert.

title: Engineering Hiring Process
description: How technical interviews are structured for engineering roles.
content: The engineering hiring process has 4 stages. Stage 1: Recruiter phone screen (30 min). Stage 2: Technical phone screen with an engineer covering fundamentals (45 min). Stage 3: Take-home project or live coding (90 min). Stage 4: Virtual on-site with 4 panels: system design technical depth values and team fit. Scoring rubric is in the interview guide. Make a hiring decision within 48 hours of the on-site. Offer approval requires VP Engineering sign-off for senior roles and above.

title: Observability Strategy
description: Company approach to logs metrics and distributed tracing.
content: The three pillars of observability are logs metrics and traces. Logs: structured JSON shipped to Elasticsearch. Metrics: Prometheus with Grafana dashboards. Traces: OpenTelemetry with Jaeger backend. All services must emit all three signal types. SLOs are defined for each critical service and tracked in Grafana. Error budgets are reviewed monthly. The platform team owns the observability infrastructure. Instrumentation libraries are provided per language.

title: Onboarding 30-60-90 Day Goals
description: Framework for setting and tracking new hire goals in the first quarter.
content: 30-day goals focus on learning: understand the team product and codebase. Meet all key stakeholders. Complete required training. 60-day goals focus on contributing: deliver first project. Identify one area for process improvement. Build confidence in tools and workflows. 90-day goals focus on performing: operate independently. Have delivered measurable impact. Align on a development plan with your manager. Review goals at each 1:1 during the first quarter.

title: Legal Hold Procedures
description: How to preserve data and documents when litigation is reasonably anticipated.
content: When legal hold is triggered by Legal email instructions to the custodians. Custodians must not delete modify or dispose of any relevant data. IT places litigation holds on email accounts and cloud storage. Legal hold supersedes normal data retention schedules. Hold status is tracked in the matter management system. When the hold is lifted Legal sends a release notice. Non-compliance with a legal hold is a serious violation with significant legal consequences.

title: Service Level Agreements (SLAs)
description: Definitions of service level targets and how they are measured.
content: Enterprise customers receive a 99.9% monthly uptime SLA. Uptime is calculated as (total minutes - downtime minutes) / total minutes. Scheduled maintenance windows are excluded. SLA credits are issued for breaches: 99.5-99.9%: 10% monthly fee credit. below 99.5%: 25% monthly fee credit. Credits must be requested within 30 days. The status page at status.company.com shows real-time and historical uptime. SLA reports are generated monthly.

title: Bias Interruption Techniques in Hiring
description: Practical techniques to reduce unconscious bias in the hiring process.
content: Use structured interviews with identical questions for all candidates. Score candidates independently before the debrief. Use a scorecard to anchor feedback on specific competencies. Avoid referencing irrelevant personal information in debriefs. Require diverse interview panels. Blind resume review is available via the Greenhouse integration. If you notice bias in a debrief speak up respectfully. Track and review demographic data at each funnel stage monthly.

title: Cryptography Standards
description: Approved cryptographic algorithms and key management practices.
content: Approved algorithms: symmetric encryption AES-256-GCM. Asymmetric RSA-4096 or ECDSA P-256. Hashing SHA-256 or SHA-3. Password hashing bcrypt argon2. Deprecated and prohibited: MD5 SHA-1 DES 3DES RC4. All cryptographic keys are managed in HashiCorp Vault. Key rotation schedule: 90 days for encryption keys. 1 year for signing keys. Document key derivation methods in architecture docs. Never implement custom cryptography.

title: Agile Retrospective Guide
description: How to run effective team retrospectives at the end of each sprint.
content: Retrospectives are held on the last day of each sprint and last 60 minutes. Use the Start Stop Continue format or a Miro board for variety. Timeboxing: 10 min check-in. 20 min generate insights. 20 min decide on actions. 10 min close. Each retro must produce at least one actionable item with an owner and due date. Previous action items are reviewed first. Rotate the facilitator role each sprint. Retro notes are stored in Confluence.

title: Ethics Hotline and Reporting
description: How to report ethical concerns or potential violations anonymously.
content: The ethics hotline is available 24/7 at 1-800-555-0199 or online at ethics.company.com. Reports can be made anonymously. Topics covered include fraud corruption safety violations and conflicts of interest. All reports are reviewed by the General Counsel. Investigations are conducted impartially. Retaliation against reporters is strictly prohibited. You will receive a confirmation code to check the status of your report. The company is committed to a speak-up culture.

title: AWS IAM Best Practices
description: How to manage AWS Identity and Access Management securely.
content: Follow the principle of least privilege for all IAM policies. Use IAM roles instead of long-lived access keys. Rotate access keys every 90 days. Enable MFA for all human IAM users especially root. Never share IAM credentials. Use AWS Organizations SCPs to enforce guardrails. Review IAM Access Advisor monthly to identify unused permissions. Document all service account roles and their purpose. Use permission boundaries to limit blast radius.

title: Customer Privacy Notice
description: How we collect use and protect customer personal data.
content: We collect personal data to provide and improve our services. Data collected includes contact information usage data and payment details. We do not sell personal data to third parties. Data is stored in EU data centres with encryption at rest and in transit. Retention periods are defined in the data retention schedule. Customers have rights to access correct delete and port their data. Submit data subject requests to privacy@company.com. Our full privacy policy is at company.com/privacy.

title: Sprint Demo Best Practices
description: How to run an effective sprint demo for stakeholders.
content: Sprint demos are held on the last Thursday of each sprint. Invite all stakeholders including product design and leadership. Demo working software not slides. Each team member demos their own work where possible. Use a staging environment for the demo. Prepare a script and rehearse. Capture feedback in Jira stories. Time limit is 60 minutes. Record the demo for stakeholders who cannot attend. Start and end on time.

title: Compliance with Export Control Laws
description: Company policy on export controls and trade compliance.
content: Certain technologies software and data are subject to export control laws including EAR and ITAR. Do not export controlled items to sanctioned countries or parties without a license. Check new customer names against the denied parties list in the trade compliance portal. Technology transfers to foreign nationals may require a deemed export licence. Contact Legal before transferring controlled technology internationally. Annual export control training is mandatory for relevant roles.

title: Performance Improvement Plan Process
description: How PIPs are initiated documented and concluded.
content: A PIP is initiated when performance consistently falls below expectations after coaching. HR must be involved from the start. The PIP documents specific performance gaps measurable improvement targets a support plan and a 30-60-90 day timeline. Weekly check-ins with HR and manager are required. Successful completion leads to removal from PIP and normal performance management. Unsuccessful completion may result in termination following HR and Legal review. All PIP documentation is retained in the personnel file.

title: Service Mesh Architecture
description: How Istio service mesh is configured and used in the Kubernetes environment.
content: Istio is deployed as the service mesh for all Kubernetes workloads. mTLS is enforced for all pod-to-pod communication. Traffic management policies use VirtualService and DestinationRule resources. Circuit breaking is configured with a threshold of 50% error rate over 30 seconds. Canary deployments use traffic splitting in the VirtualService. Observability: Kiali for topology Jaeger for traces Prometheus for metrics. Service mesh configuration is in the istio-config repository.

title: Annual Budget Planning Process
description: How the company plans and approves the annual operating budget.
content: Budget planning starts in September for the following fiscal year. Department heads submit budget proposals by October 1st. Finance consolidates and models scenarios. Executive review in November finalises the budget. Board approval in December. Budgets are loaded into Adaptive Planning by January 1st. Monthly variance reports compare actual vs budget. Budget amendments above $50000 require CFO approval. Headcount changes require separate approval from HR and the relevant VP.

title: Pair Programming Guide
description: When and how to use pair programming effectively.
content: Pair programming is encouraged for complex features unfamiliar codebases and knowledge transfer. Roles: Driver types the code. Navigator reviews and thinks ahead. Swap roles every 25 minutes using Pomodoro timing. Use VS Code Live Share for remote pairing sessions. Pairing sessions should not exceed 2 hours without a break. Communicate your thinking aloud. Respect your pair's working style. Pair programming counts as a code review so separate PR review may not be required.

title: Intellectual Property Assignment
description: How employee inventions and work products are assigned to the company.
content: Work products created during employment and within the scope of your role are company property. This includes code designs inventions and written works. Work created entirely on personal time with no company resources may be excluded submit an IP Disclosure form to Legal to clarify. Inventions related to company business created during employment are generally assigned to the company. Review your employment agreement for the specific IP assignment clause. Contact Legal with questions.
